conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, could you tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 16 years old, and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me gather some details about your and Alex's situation.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, full-time non-advanced education such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved unpaid training)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's studying for his A-levels at the local sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. 

**Does Alex live with you, and are you responsible for him?** (This means you're the main person looking after him and paying for things like food, clothes, and other costs)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives with me at home, and I'm responsible for him. I pay for everything he needs - his food, clothes, school supplies, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) subject to immigration control, or do you have any restrictions on accessing public funds in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there are no immigration restrictions. I live in the UK and have full access to public funds.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question:

**Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for Alex, or is anyone else trying to claim for him?** (For example, if he splits time between two households, or if someone else has claimed for him)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me - he lives with me full-time and I'm the only one responsible for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me and the official Child Benefit rules, I can now provide you with a decision:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Although Alex is 16 years old (above the normal age limit of under 16), you still qualify for Child Benefit because:

- Alex is under 20 years old
- He is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college)
- He lives with you
- You are responsible for him and pay for his needs
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- You have no immigration restrictions

**Please note:** If Alex leaves his A-level studies, you may still be able to receive Child Benefit for up to 20 weeks if he registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. Child Benefit will stop if Alex starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training, or if he starts receiving certain benefits in his own right.

Also be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college). Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, but eligibility extends to children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex lives with you, you are responsible for him, and you pay for his needs (food, clothes, school supplies). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. You have no immigration restrictions and have full access to public funds in the UK. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 16), who is studying A-levels at sixth form college. The claimant lives with Alex, is responsible for him, pays for all his needs, and no one else is claiming for him. The claimant has no immigration restrictions. As Alex is in approved education and under 20 years old, and all other eligibility criteria are met, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Alex lives with you, you are responsible for him, and you pay for his needs (food, clothes, school supplies). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. You have no immigration restrictions and have full access to public funds in the UK. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

The claimant lives with Alex, is responsible for him, pays for all his needs, and no one else is claiming for him. The claimant has no immigration restrictions. As Alex is in approved education and under 20 years old, and all other eligibility criteria are met, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
